commands.review_summary:
The PR adds role-specific memory plugin slots, per-agent memory slot overrides, legacy recall migration/diagnostics, and wiring across plugin activation, memory runtime resolution, doctor/status, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a broken-existing-behavior report. The review path is code/schema/proof inspection rather than reproducing a current-main bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer API/product approval and contributor proof refresh, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Exact-head real behavior proof refresh for PR #88504 multi-slot memory role architecture at `fdeb555009b79e2810ee93304e430030ab6db59d`. This proof covers upgrade/install over the last public release, deterministic build/type/package gates, global and per-agent memory role configuration, Honcho health, active-memory capture transcript generation, and five real GitHub Copilot agent turns across distinct memory plugin combinations.
- Real environment tested: Orion / Nano-01 at `192.168.1.117`, host `Nano-01`, Linux aarch64 Jetson kernel `5.15.185-tegra`, checkout `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-88504`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`. Baseline rollback target: public release `v2026.5.28` at `e93216080aa1f425d3ab127014603eba8e365b2d`. Candidate installed after proof package upgrade: `OpenClaw 2026.5.31 (fdeb555)`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: On Nano-01, preserved pre-proof dirty work as a stash, checked out `v2026.5.28`, captured baseline `config validate`, `doctor`, `status`, `plugins list`, and `models status`; checked out exact PR head `fdeb555009b79e2810ee93304e430030ab6db59d`; ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm build:strict-smoke`, `pnpm tsgo:prod`, `git diff --check`, `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`, candidate `openclaw config validate --json`, `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes`, `openclaw status --json`, `openclaw plugins list --json`, `openclaw models status --json`, `npm pack --json`; installed the generated tarball globally over the prior global OpenClaw; expanded active-memory's test allow-list to all five proof agents; validated config again; ran real `openclaw agent --json` turns for `main`, `honcho-agent`, `dreamer-agent`, `split-agent`, and `disabled-recall-agent` using GitHub Copilot.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Redacted/captured proof artifacts are on Orion at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/proof/pr88504-proof-20260601T002337Z`; compact parsed summary is `logs/70-proof-summary.json`; active-memory transcript proof is `logs/66-active-memory-transcript-proof.log`; package artifact is `artifacts/openclaw-2026.5.31.tgz` with sha256 `d268f7e36d3621295d07472e5642d9a246a0d75ff95f3fa62ea0d667de10c84b`.

```text
head=fdeb555009b79e2810ee93304e430030ab6db59d
run_id=pr88504-proof-20260601T002337Z
baseline_release=v2026.5.28 e93216080aa1f425d3ab127014603eba8e365b2d
candidate_version=OpenClaw 2026.5.31 (fdeb555)
package_sha256=d268f7e36d3621295d07472e5642d9a246a0d75ff95f3fa62ea0d667de10c84b  openclaw-2026.5.31.tgz
install_over_baseline_before=OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (c955371)
install_over_baseline_after=OpenClaw 2026.5.31 (fdeb555)
config_validate={"valid":true,"path":"/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/openclaw.json"}
honcho_health={"status":"ok"}
gateway_probe=Reachable: yes; Capability: connected-no-operator-scope
```

```text
deterministic_gates:
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile: passed
- pnpm build:strict-smoke: passed
- pnpm tsgo:prod: passed
- git diff --check: passed-empty
- node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs: passed-empty
- openclaw config validate --json: valid=true
- openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes: completed; expected local gateway-token warning; gateway service not installed on test host
- openclaw status/plugins/models diagnostics: completed; runtimeVersion=2026.5.31; github-copilot auth profile present
- npm pack --json: passed; package copied and hashed
- npm install -g openclaw-2026.5.31.tgz --prefix ~/.npm-global: passed
```

```text
role_slot_matrix_after_active_memory_allowlist_expansion:
main: global memory.recall=memory-core, memory.dreaming=memory-core, memory.userModel=openclaw-honcho, contextEngine=lossless-claw
honcho-agent: memory.recall=openclaw-honcho, memory.userModel=openclaw-honcho
dreamer-agent: memory.recall=memory-core, memory.dreaming=memory-core, memory.userModel=openclaw-honcho
split-agent: memory.recall=memory-core, memory.capture=active-memory, memory.userModel=openclaw-honcho
disabled-recall-agent: memory.recall=none, memory.capture=active-memory, memory.userModel=openclaw-honcho
active-memory config.agents=[main,honcho-agent,dreamer-agent,split-agent,disabled-recall-agent]
```

```text
real_github_copilot_live_turns:
main: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, tools=[memory_search], failures=0, LIVE_PROOF_OK
honcho-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, tools=[honcho_search_messages,memory_search,session_status], failures=0, LIVE_PROOF_OK
dreamer-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, tools=[session_status,memory_search], failures=0, LIVE_PROOF_OK
split-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, tools=[memory_search], failures=0, LIVE_CAPTURE_PROOF_OK, active-memory transcript file created
disabled-recall-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, tools=[memory_search], failures=0, LIVE_CAPTURE_PROOF_OK, active-memory transcript file created
```

```text
active_memory_transcript_files_created_2026-06-01T01:00Z_to_01:04Z:
main/active-memory-proof/active-memory-mpui4aha-3305702e.jsonl
honcho-agent/active-memory-proof/active-memory-mpui4yus-4911ce16.jsonl
dreamer-agent/active-memory-proof/active-memory-mpui5qd4-304f1309.jsonl
split-agent/active-memory-proof/active-memory-mpui9qck-183d2efc.jsonl
disabled-recall-agent/active-memory-proof/active-memory-mpuiag6t-6db920b5.jsonl
sentinel=PR88504 ACTIVE_MEMORY_CAPTURE_SENTINEL present in capture-expanded split/disabled proof turns
```

- Observed result after fix: Exact-head candidate built, typechecked, packaged, installed globally over the previous OpenClaw install, validated config, completed doctor/status/plugin/model diagnostics, confirmed Honcho health, and completed seven real GitHub Copilot agent turns without timeout: one smoke turn, five role-matrix turns, plus two capture-expanded turns for the active-memory capture roles. The previous ClawSweeper blocker where four role-heavy live turns timed out is superseded by positive exit-code-0 live-turn proof at `fdeb555009b79e2810ee93304e430030ab6db59d`.
- What was not tested: Windows and macOS live install paths were not tested. A full scheduled/background dreaming maintenance cycle was not forced; the proof validates the configured `memory.dreaming` slot and exercises live recall/search plus active-memory capture paths. The `openclaw agent` command reported `runner=embedded` in the live-turn metadata even after a loopback Gateway probe succeeded, so this proof does not claim Gateway-run live turns.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The Orion host does not have an installed Gateway service; a temporary foreground Gateway was started for probe evidence and then stopped. `disabled-recall-agent` still saw `memory_search` from the broader context/search tool surface, so this proof treats `memory.recall=none` as config-scope proof rather than proof that every search-like tool disappears. The memory role taxonomy and legacy migration window remain public API decisions for maintainer signoff.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current PR branch head:

- `fdeb555009b79e2810ee93304e430030ab6db59d` (`fix(gateway): preserve session info context tokens`)
- Branch: `kklouzal:feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`
- Base target: `openclaw/openclaw:main`
- Prior stale proof heads superseded by this refresh included `440883ce09dae793ee9f47a29d35eb90be4f095c`, `c9553718a65697ecb75ffb64f37245856fca0eb7`, and `da7ea0f3d2bd240ec4e9cefcd512e0675489f9fb`.

Exact-head Orion / Nano-01 proof artifact:

- `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/proof/pr88504-proof-20260601T002337Z`
- Compact parsed proof summary: `logs/70-proof-summary.json`
- Active-memory transcript proof: `logs/66-active-memory-transcript-proof.log`
- Host/runtime: Nano-01 / Orion, aarch64 Linux, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- Baseline rollback target: `v2026.5.28` at `e93216080aa1f425d3ab127014603eba8e365b2d`.
- Installed candidate after package upgrade: `OpenClaw 2026.5.31 (fdeb555)`.
- Candidate package: `artifacts/openclaw-2026.5.31.tgz`.
- Package sha256: `d268f7e36d3621295d07472e5642d9a246a0d75ff95f3fa62ea0d667de10c84b`.

Exact-head proof gates:

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`: passed.
- `pnpm build:strict-smoke`: passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`: passed.
- `openclaw config validate --json`: passed.
- `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes`: completed with expected local gateway-token warning and no installed Gateway service on the test host.
- Installed status, plugin list, and model-status diagnostics: completed; runtime version `2026.5.31`; GitHub Copilot auth profile present.
- `npm pack --json`: passed and produced `openclaw-2026.5.31.tgz`.
- Global npm install over the prior installed OpenClaw: passed; version changed from `OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (c955371)` to `OpenClaw 2026.5.31 (fdeb555)`.
- Honcho direct health probe: passed at `127.0.0.1:8000/health` with `{"status":"ok"}`.
- Five-agent role matrix config validation: passed for `main`, `honcho-agent`, `dreamer-agent`, `split-agent`, and `disabled-recall-agent`.
- Real GitHub Copilot live turns: passed for all five role-matrix agents; no live-turn timeouts in the refreshed proof.
- Active-memory capture proof: transcript files created for all five proof agents after expanding the active-memory proof allow-list to all five agents.

Live proof caveats:

- `openclaw agent` metadata reported `runner=embedded` even after loopback Gateway probe succeeded, so the proof intentionally does not claim Gateway-run live turns.
- `disabled-recall-agent` still had `memory_search` available from the broader context/search tool surface, so `memory.recall=none` is claimed as config-scope behavior, not absence of every search-like tool.
- The proof validates the configured `memory.dreaming` slot and live recall/search/capture paths, but does not force a full scheduled/background dreaming maintenance cycle.
